interface: Vault HTTP API v1 — Transit secrets engine batch encrypt/decrypt (stable)

request_headers.X-Vault-Token: the client token, on every request
request_headers.X-Vault-Namespace: namespace path such as eng/payments/, on every request when configured

key_name_escaping: the key name is a URL path segment and must be RFC 3986 percent-escaped: space -> %20, '#' -> %23, non-ASCII as UTF-8 percent-escaped bytes
form encoding is wrong here: '+' in the path is a literal plus, never a space

rules: plaintext, context, and associated_data are all base64-encoded byte strings
optional per-item fields (context, associated_data, key_version) are omitted from the JSON entirely when not set
context is the key-derivation context; associated_data is additionally authenticated data for AEAD ciphers
key_version pins which key version encrypts the item; unset means the latest version
without partial_failure_response_code Vault answers a batch containing any failed item with HTTP 400; sending 200 makes partial results decodable from a success response
ciphertext carries the vault:vN: prefix where N is the encrypting key version; key_version reports the same version
a failed item carries only an error string; sibling items keep their results

decrypt_response.rule: plaintext is base64 and must be decoded back to the original bytes (they may be binary, not text)

request_level_errors.rule: a non-2xx response (e.g. 400 encryption key not found, 403 permission denied) decodes into the transport's structured exception with status and the full errors array; per-item errors never raise
